title: 8.6 practice
description: Practice what you learned in lesson 8.6!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did many people in Europe support appeasement policies during the time they were implemented?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They feared another devastating war and hoped to maintain peace.
They believed Germany would be a better ally than an enemy.
They did not think Germany’s expansion posed a serious threat.
They wanted to support the rise of authoritarian governments in Europe.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Before Germany invaded Poland, how did Britain and France typically respond to German aggression?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They took no direct action to stop Hitler’s aggression.
They declared war immediately to stop German expansion.
They encouraged Germany to continue expanding eastward.
They allied with the Soviet Union against Germany.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How have modern politicians often used Neville Chamberlain’s failed appeasement of Hitler to justify military action?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They argue that avoiding conflicts allows aggressors to become more dangerous.
They promise to “never again” allow obvious violations of human rights to continue.
They promote the idea that diplomacy is always more effective than war.
They defend Chamberlain’s strategy as a successful model for modern foreign policy.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which event demonstrated Germany’s military dominance in the early years of World War II?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The defeat of France in 1940
The invasion of the Soviet Union
The Allied invasion of Normandy (D-Day)
The bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki

assessment_items.question_content:
****Which key events signaled a turning point in World War II in favor of the Allies?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Hitler’s invasion of the Soviet Union and the Soviet Union’s entry into the war on the Allied side
Japan’s attack on Pearl Harbor and the U.S. entrance into the war
Germany’s invasion of Poland, which prompted France and Britain to declare war
Germany’s defeat of France and the addition of Italy to the Axis powers

assessment_items.question_content:
**How were codebreakers significant to the Allied victory in World War II?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They gave Allied forces a strategic advantage by intercepting and decoding enemy messages.
They were responsible for building the atomic bomb which was used to end the war in the Pacific.
They repaired broken communication equipment in the trenches, ensuring Allied success in battles.
Historians believe code breaking lengthened the war by 1-2 years, giving the Allied forces time to change the tide.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What advantage did the Soviet Union have when it came to directing resources toward a total war economy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Soviet government already had total command over all industries.
The Soviet Union, in particular, received help from the United States.
The Soviet Union’s takeover of land in Poland provided it with resources.
The Soviet Union was able to take resources from its colonies.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How did World War II affect the American economy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It led to the end of the Great Depression and boosted industrial growth.
American manufacturing could not keep up with the supplies needed to support the war.
It forced the United States to rely entirely on foreign goods and aid.
It caused a deep recession and widespread unemployment.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How did Germany's economic situation after World War I contribute to Hitler's rise to power?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Widespread unemployment and inflation made people desperate for strong leadership.
International trade boomed, allowing Germany to recover without conflict.
Economic prosperity made people more interested in expanding democracy.
Reparations from the Treaty of Versailles had little effect on the German economy.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best explains why some historians view the use of atomic bombs on Japan as a more humane way to end World War II?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The alternatives would have resulted in even greater civilian casualties.
The bombs caused less destruction than traditional warfare.
It prevented the Soviet Union from entering the war in the Pacific.
It gave the United States an opportunity to demonstrate its power to the Soviet Union.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did some Japanese leaders view the Soviet Union’s entry into the war as a greater threat than the atomic bombings?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They feared the Soviets would occupy parts of the country, resulting in communist control.
The atomic bombs caused little physical damage compared to previous attacks.
They believed the Soviet Union would offer peace terms that were more favorable to Japan.
The Soviet Union had a stronger military than the United States.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Besides the destruction and loss of life, what was another major global consequence of dropping the atomic bombs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It started a global nuclear arms race.
It resulted in the collapse of the United Nations.
It led to the immediate end of all international conflicts.
It decreased the United States influence in international affairs.
